preamble: The development of an allophonic glottal coda [ʔ] in historically open syllables is a precursor to Tonogenesis in several East Asian languages. This process, often occurring in short syllables, provides a distinctive closure that eventually influences the pitch contour and leads to the formation of 'Checked Tones'.
phoneticEffects: Coda Addition, Glottalization, Tonogenesis, Checked Tone

examples.note: Early stage where short vowels developed a glottal closure, leading to the Sắc/Hỏi tone categories.
Allophonic glottal closure in short syllables as a precursor to tone stabilization.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
